commit.message:
fix: use implicit flow for password reset email in Payt webhook

The previous PKCE-based resetPasswordForEmail was generating a code_verifier
on the server (webhook context) that was never accessible to the buyer's
browser, causing exchangeCodeForSession to fail and redirect to login.

Switching to flowType:'implicit' sends hash-based tokens in the email link
(#access_token=...&type=recovery), which the /auth/callback page already
handles correctly via onAuthStateChange → PASSWORD_RECOVERY event.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add Phase 1 NumPy parity ops across all backends

New unary ops:
- arcsinh, arccosh, arctanh (inverse hyperbolic)
- expm1, log1p (numerically stable for small x)
- trunc, fix (round toward zero)
- sinc, deg2rad, rad2deg
- heaviside, signbit

New decomposition ops:
- modf (fractional and integral parts)
- frexp (mantissa and exponent)
- ldexp (mantissa * 2^exp)
- divmod (floor division and modulo)

New binary ops:
- mod, fmod, remainder
- copysign, hypot, arctan2
- logaddexp, logaddexp2
- fmax, fmin (NaN-ignoring)

All ops implemented for JS, WASM, and WebGPU backends with
parameterized tests (480 tests passing).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add AWQ int4→bf16 dequantization programming example (#1357)

* Add AWQ int4→bf16 dequantization programming example

Implement AWQ-style int4 to bfloat16 dequantization with per-group
scale and zero-point parameters. Uses external C++ kernel that
unpacks int4 values from uint8 pairs and applies the dequant formula:
  output[i] = (int4_weight[i] - zero_point[group]) * scale[group]

Scales and zero-points are interleaved into a single params buffer
to stay within the DMA channel limit (2 S2MM + 1 MM2S).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Material Icons: remove font-family from root, add JS icon fixer

Root cause: .stApp { font-family: 'DM Sans' !important } cascaded to
ALL children via CSS inheritance, overriding Material Icons on expander
arrows and sidebar nav icons. Class-based CSS selectors couldn't match
because Streamlit's icon spans may not have the expected class names.

Two-layer fix:
- CSS: removed font-family from root .stApp rule (kept background only),
  narrowed typography selector to explicit text elements only, added
  broad Material Icons protection with position-based selectors
- JS: MutationObserver detects spans containing known icon names
  (arrow_right, expand_more, etc.) and forces Material Icons font
  via inline style.setProperty — bulletproof regardless of class names

Co-Authored-By: Claude (claude-opus-4-6) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

server: support provider/model selectors and optional provider field (#114)

* providers: preserve provider instance identity in registry cache

* server: support provider/model selectors and optional provider field

* providers: remove cache versioning

Backward compatibility is not a concern, so the version field and
mismatch guard in the model cache are unnecessary complexity.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* cache: group models by provider and fix slash-in-model-ID lookups

Restructure ModelCache to group models by provider, eliminating
per-model repetition of provider_type, owned_by, and object fields.
Drop the always-"model" Object field entirely.

Fix splitModelSelector fallthrough: when a qualified lookup fails
(e.g. "meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-70B" where "meta-llama" is not a
provider name), fall through to unqualified lookup using the original
model string instead of returning nil. Applied to GetProvider,
GetModel, Supports, and GetProviderType.

Harden cache tests with fatal guards before slice indexing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: refactored the code to remove repetitions

* fix: resolve pre-existing lint issues in contract test suite

Remove unused loadGoldenFile generic function, apply De Morgan's law
(QF1001), and use strings.TrimPrefix instead of HasPrefix+slice (S1017).

commit.message:
fix: prevent phantom post-resolve close after graduated auto-close

The set_post_resolve_close trigger was setting a new auto_close_at
when the cron auto-closed a ticket after 3 follow-ups, causing a
4th unnecessary "Glad we could help!" message. Now skips the
post-resolve window when follow_up_count >= 3 since those tickets
already completed the full graduated lifecycle.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add x402 USDC per-API-call pricing and Stripe metered billing (#18)

Implements dual payment rails for Kontext API: Stripe metered billing ($2/1K
events above 20K free tier) and x402 USDC micropayments on Base for usage above
the free tier. Both rails charge identical pricing, allowing users to choose their
preferred payment method. Includes Circle Programmable Wallet integration for USDC
settlement and self-dogfooding compliance logging.

Changes:
- Server: Replace $449/month per-seat Stripe subscription with metered billing
  at $2/1K events; add reportUsage() function for metered tracking
- x402: New middleware for HTTP 402 Payment Required responses, facilitator
  verification via Coinbase, and payment settlement to Circle Programmable Wallet
- SDK: New x402-client.ts with createX402Fetch() factory for optional client-side
  payment handling; uses dynamic imports to avoid bundling peer dependencies
- Website: Promote 8-tool MCP server across agent tab, pricing, and homepage;
  add x402 USDC payment option to Pro tier; update x402 FAQ and pricing copy
- Tests: 36 Stripe metered billing tests + 11 x402 middleware tests (53 total pass)

Free tier: 20,000 events/month hard limit on Base + Arc, $0 forever
Pro tier: Unlimited events via Stripe metered billing or USDC on Base via x402
Price: $2.00/1K events above free tier ($0.002/event via x402)
